exercises.title: Identify OWASP vulnerabilities
exercises.instructions:
Which OWASP Top 10 category does each belong to?
A: POST /orders body includes adminFlag=true
B: SELECT * FROM users WHERE name=''+userInput+''
C: Storing API key in git repository
D: /api/reports/../../../etc/passwd
exercises.expected_solution:
A: A04 Insecure Design / Mass Assignment
   DTO directly maps Entity fields → client sets adminFlag via JSON
   Fix: separate DTO (record OrderRequest) — no adminFlag field

B: A03 Injection (SQL Injection)
   String concatenation into SQL → attacker injects OR '1'='1'
   Fix: JPA @Query with @Param (PreparedStatement binding)

C: A02 Cryptographic Failures / Secret Exposure
   API key in git → accessible to everyone with repo access
   Fix: Vault KV store, environment variables, GitHub Secrets

D: A01 Broken Access Control (Path Traversal)
   ../../../etc/passwd: escape the intended directory
   Fix: validate file paths, use allowlist, never pass user input to File() directly

test_cases.expected:
A: A04 Insecure Design / Mass Assignment
   DTO directly maps Entity fields → client sets adminFlag via JSON
   Fix: separate DTO (record OrderRequest) — no adminFlag field

B: A03 Injection (SQL Injection)
   String concatenation into SQL → attacker injects OR '1'='1'
   Fix: JPA @Query with @Param (PreparedStatement binding)

C: A02 Cryptographic Failures / Secret Exposure
   API key in git → accessible to everyone with repo access
   Fix: Vault KV store, environment variables, GitHub Secrets

D: A01 Broken Access Control (Path Traversal)
   ../../../etc/passwd: escape the intended directory
   Fix: validate file paths, use allowlist, never pass user input to File() directly

hints: A01: access control failures (most common)
A03: injection (SQL, NoSQL, command)
Mass assignment: client controls entity fields directly

exercises.title: Complete the @Valid DTO
exercises.instructions: Fill in validation annotations for a payment amount field.
hints: @NotNull: field must not be null
@DecimalMin: minimum value for BigDecimal/Number fields

exercises.title: @Valid controller + global exception handler
exercises.instructions:
Write:
1. PaymentRequest record with @Valid constraints
2. PaymentController with @Valid @RequestBody
3. @RestControllerAdvice returning 400 with field errors
4. @WebMvcTest: invalid amount → 400 with error message
hints: @RestControllerAdvice: global exception handler for all controllers
MethodArgumentNotValidException: thrown by @Valid on failed validation
jsonPath("$.amount").exists(): verifies field error is returned

exercises.title: Custom @Constraint validator
exercises.instructions:
Write a custom validator @ValidTicker:
1. Checks that a stock symbol exists in a cached list (NSE symbols)
2. ConstraintValidator<ValidTicker, String>
3. Apply to OrderRequest.symbol field
4. @SpringBootTest: invalid symbol → validation error
hints: @Constraint(validatedBy=...): links annotation to validator implementation
isValid: return true = valid, false = invalid
Validator bean: auto-configured by Spring Boot — inject directly

exercises.title: Prevent mass assignment with DTO pattern
exercises.instructions:
Show the mass assignment problem and fix:
1. Vulnerable: @RequestBody PaymentEntity (JPA entity)
2. Secure: @RequestBody PaymentRequest (DTO with safe fields only)
3. Entity has: id, merchantId, amount, status, adminOverride, balance
4. DTO only exposes: merchantId, amount, gateway
5. @SpringBootTest: extra fields in JSON silently ignored
hints: @JsonIgnoreProperties(ignoreUnknown=true): silently ignore JSON fields not in DTO
DTO must NOT contain sensitive entity fields
Mapping: DTO → Entity manually in service (not auto-mapping)

exercises.title: Security headers with Spring Security
exercises.instructions:
Configure Spring Security security headers:
1. Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'
2. X-Frame-Options: DENY (clickjacking)
3. X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
4. HSTS: max-age=31536000
5. @WebMvcTest: verify headers present in response
hints: Spring Security: applies security headers by default on all responses
X-Frame-Options: DENY prevents clickjacking (page can't be embedded in iframe)
CSP: browser-level XSS protection — only execute scripts from 'self' (your domain)

exercises.title: SQL injection prevention verification
exercises.instructions:
Write tests proving SQL injection is prevented:
1. JPA @Query with @Param (parameterized)
2. Test: injection attempt ' OR '1'='1 returns nothing (not all records)
3. Native query: also parameterized (not concatenated)
4. Explain: why PreparedStatement prevents injection
hints: PreparedStatement: input is data, not SQL — structure fixed at compile time
JPA @Query with @Param: always uses PreparedStatement
The SQL template is precompiled: user input can never change query structure

exercises.title: Path traversal prevention
exercises.instructions:
Write FileSafetyService preventing path traversal:
1. downloadReport(String filename): user requests a report
2. Validate: filename cannot contain '..' or '/'
3. Resolve against allowed base directory
4. If path escapes base dir: throw SecurityException
5. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): safe path OK, traversal blocked
hints: normalize(): resolves .. components in the path
startsWith(BASE_DIR): verify the resolved path is still under the allowed directory
contains('..'): fast rejection before even resolving

exercises.title: XSS prevention with sanitization
exercises.instructions:
Write XSSProtectionService:
1. sanitizeHtml(String input): strips dangerous HTML tags
2. Use OWASP Java HTML Sanitizer or Jsoup clean
3. Allow: p, b, i, em, strong (text formatting)
4. Block: script, onclick, onerror, iframe
5. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): safe HTML preserved, XSS stripped
hints: Jsoup.clean: whitelist-based HTML sanitizer (allowlist approach)
Safelist.basic(): allows safe formatting tags
Thymeleaf: escapes by default (th:text escapes; th:utext does NOT — be careful)

exercises.title: What validation error is returned?
exercises.expected_solution:
A: {symbol: '', quantity: -5, currency: 'us'}
   symbol: 'must not be blank' (violates @NotBlank)
   quantity: 'must be greater than 0' (violates @Positive, value=-5)
   currency: 'must match [A-Z]{3}' (violates @Pattern, 'us' is lowercase)
   Total: 3 validation errors → 400 Bad Request

B: {symbol: 'RELIANCE_TECHNOLOGIES', quantity: 10}
   symbol: 'size must be between 2 and 10' (violates @Size, length=20)
   currency: null → no @NotNull on currency → passes (null is OK!)
   Total: 1 validation error → 400 Bad Request
   Note: @Pattern doesn't apply to null values (unless @NotNull is also present)

C: {symbol: 'TCS', quantity: 10, currency: 'USD'}
   All constraints satisfied:
   symbol 'TCS': not blank, length=3 (2-10 range) ✓
   quantity 10: positive, ≤ 10000 ✓
   currency 'USD': matches [A-Z]{3} ✓
   Total: 0 validation errors → passes validation

test_cases.expected:
A: {symbol: '', quantity: -5, currency: 'us'}
   symbol: 'must not be blank' (violates @NotBlank)
   quantity: 'must be greater than 0' (violates @Positive, value=-5)
   currency: 'must match [A-Z]{3}' (violates @Pattern, 'us' is lowercase)
   Total: 3 validation errors → 400 Bad Request

B: {symbol: 'RELIANCE_TECHNOLOGIES', quantity: 10}
   symbol: 'size must be between 2 and 10' (violates @Size, length=20)
   currency: null → no @NotNull on currency → passes (null is OK!)
   Total: 1 validation error → 400 Bad Request
   Note: @Pattern doesn't apply to null values (unless @NotNull is also present)

C: {symbol: 'TCS', quantity: 10, currency: 'USD'}
   All constraints satisfied:
   symbol 'TCS': not blank, length=3 (2-10 range) ✓
   quantity 10: positive, ≤ 10000 ✓
   currency 'USD': matches [A-Z]{3} ✓
   Total: 0 validation errors → passes validation

hints: @Pattern: doesn't apply to null — add @NotNull if null is not OK
@NotBlank: implies not null AND not empty
Multiple annotations: all must pass — failure on any → error for that field

exercises.title: Complete Zerodha order validation stack
exercises.instructions:
Build complete Zerodha order validation:
1. OrderRequest record with @ValidTicker + @Positive @Max(10000)
2. OrderController with @Valid + @PreAuthorize('hasRole(TRADER)')
3. Validation exception handler
4. @WebMvcTest (3 tests): valid order→200, invalid symbol→400, no auth→401
5. All 3 pass
hints: Combine @Valid + @PreAuthorize: validation runs before business logic, auth runs before validation
jsonPath('$.symbol').exists(): verifies field error returned for that field
No JWT → 401 (before @Valid runs — security check first)

exercises.title: Content Security Policy for Razorpay dashboard
exercises.instructions:
Configure comprehensive security headers for Razorpay merchant dashboard:
1. CSP: allow scripts from self + cdn.razorpay.com only
2. Block: eval, inline scripts
3. X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN (allow own iframes for dashboard)
4. HSTS: 1 year
5. @WebMvcTest: verify all headers present
hints: CSP: whitelist of allowed sources per content type
script-src: only execute scripts from your domain + trusted CDN
object-src 'none': block Flash, Java applets, plugins

exercises.title: Complete OWASP input validation suite
exercises.instructions:
Build complete input validation for Razorpay:
1. PaymentRequest DTO: @NotNull amount, @Pattern merchantId, @Size description
2. FileSafetyService: path traversal prevention
3. XSSProtectionService: sanitize description field
4. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): validation errors, path blocked, XSS stripped
5. All 3 pass
hints: Simple XSS regex: portable without Jsoup dependency in tests
Path.normalize() + startsWith: canonical path traversal check
validator.validate(): test Bean Validation without Spring MVC layer

exercises.title: CSRF protection for form-based endpoints
exercises.instructions:
Razorpay merchant dashboard has form-based endpoints (not REST/JWT):
1. Explain when CSRF protection is needed vs when to disable
2. Spring Security CSRF with double-submit cookie pattern
3. Thymeleaf: auto-include _csrf token in forms
4. REST API vs Web App: when to enable/disable
5. @SpringBootTest: CSRF test
hints: CSRF: only needed when auth is in cookies (browser auto-sends)
JWT in Authorization header: CSRF impossible (attacker can't forge headers cross-origin)
CookieCsrfTokenRepository: stores CSRF token in JS-readable cookie (for SPA apps)

exercises.title: Complete M23 T4 input validation synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete M23 T4 synthesis:
1. PaymentRequest DTO with validation constraints
2. FileSafetyService + XSSProtectionService
3. SecurityFilterChain with CSP headers
4. @WebMvcTest + @SpringBootTest (3 tests): validation 400, path blocked, CSP header present
5. All 3 pass
exercises.starter_code: // Complete M23 T4 input validation synthesis
hints: @WebMvcTest tests: validation and security headers
@SpringBootTest test: FileSafety path traversal block
CSP header: andExpect(header().string(...)) — check header value
